§6Heavy Steam: Boilers & Turbines§f

§aBoil water and spin massive turbines to generate high-capacity industrial power!§f
For heavy industrial machinery, solar and wind power are not enough. You must harness thermodynamic steam systems to generate high-voltage electricity.

§6Steam Boilers§f:
The Steam Boiler consumes water and fuel (solid or liquid) to generate high-pressure steam:
§b• Conversion Rate§f: Converts water to steam at a §e1:5 ratio§f.
§b• Water Intake§f: Consumes §e76 mB of water per tick§f.
§b• Steam Output§f: Generates §e380 mB of steam per tick§f.
§b• Solid Fuel Penalty§f: Solid fuels burn twice as fast inside the boiler firebox compared to a standard furnace (e.g., a piece of Coal provides §c800 ticks§f of firebox heat instead of 1,600 ticks).

§6Steam Turbines§f:
Feed high-pressure steam from your boiler directly into a §bSteam Turbine§f to generate electricity:
§b• Power Output§f: Produces a massive §e512 FE/tick§f at full speed.
§b• Steam Consumption§f: Requires §e250 mB of steam per tick§f to maintain maximum rotation.
§b• Water Condensation§f: As the turbine spins, it cools the steam back into liquid form, allowing you to recycle the output §bWater§f back into your boiler system.
